- **RESOLVED ISSUE** 2026-04-10 blocker-history summary: this shared repo-list task hit the same unrelated full `server:unit` `flows.run.loop` cleanup seam twice. The first full-wrapper failure was re-owned to earlier Task 62 and closed that branch at the file-level boundary, but a later full-suite rerun on `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-10T02-07-39-714Z.log` proved that closure was a false green because inflight cleanup drained while ownership stayed pinned. Planner repair first moved the active prerequisite owner to Task 64, and the current repair narrows that prerequisite again to Task 63, so this task no longer carries unrelated flow-runtime diagnosis.
- Planner repair on 2026-04-10: inserted new Task 63 ahead of Task 64 because the repeated full-wrapper blocker proved the prerequisite cleanup owner had to be reopened or replaced again at a tighter seam. This task returns to `__to_do__` with its completed implementation subtasks and build checkpoints preserved; Testing 3 through 6 remain the next proof gates after Tasks 63 through 65 restore the full `server:unit` baseline again.
- 2026-04-10 implementation-only audit: re-read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and this exact Task 69 section from disk, rechecked current repo evidence through commits `50050f3f`, `09fbc0e2`, and `e723105e`, and confirmed Subtasks 1 through 24 plus Testing 1 and 2 already honestly match current disk state. No `Testing` items were newly checked in this audit, no live `**BLOCKER**` note remained at that point, and Testing 3 through 6 were still the remaining full-wrapper and browser-proof gates before the new upstream contradiction forced planner repair.
- **RESOLVED ISSUE** 2026-04-10 Testing 3 (`npm run test:summary:server:unit`) failed before any Task 69 repo-list owner could be revalidated. I ran only that unchecked wrapper and inspected `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-10T05-40-23-161Z.log`; the failure is again the unrelated `flow stop during a looped flow prevents later iterations from continuing`, with `ownershipRelease.branch === "never_reached"` and `stopUnwind.totalCheckpoints === 20` while repeated `runFlowInstruction.returnResult` checkpoints still stop below the outer cleanup boundaries. The exact contradiction is that Task 66 had just been audited closed as a restored ownership-release prerequisite, but the first full wrapper for Task 69 reproduced the same prerequisite failure before any shared repo-list proof owners were exercised. Planner repair on 2026-04-10 resolved the task-shape defect by moving the active owner to new Task 67 instead of sending this repo-list task back into unrelated `flows.run.loop` diagnosis.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** Fresh blocker research on 2026-04-10 proves Task 69 is not the active owner and the next honest move is planner repair that reopens a flow-runtime prerequisite ahead of the shared repo-list work. Repository precedents first: `code_info` plus direct inspection of Story 43, Story 46, Story 50, and this Story 55 task chain all show the same rule in this repo: when a full-wrapper proof fails before the active task's named proof owners are exercised, the blocker is re-owned to the upstream prerequisite instead of widening the downstream task. Current local evidence matches that pattern. Task 69's implementation commit `50050f3f` touched only shared repo-list and reader files (`server/src/lmstudio/toolService.ts`, repo-list tests, `client/src/hooks/useIngestRoots.ts`, `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx`, `client/src/components/ingest/RootDetailsDrawer.tsx`, related client tests, and `e2e/ingest.spec.ts`) and did not touch `server/src/flows/service.ts`, `server/src/test/integration/flows.run.loop.test.ts`, `server/src/agents/runLock.ts`, `server/src/chat/inflightRegistry.ts`, or `server/src/test/support/wsClient.ts`. The first unchecked full-wrapper rerun for this task at `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-10T05-40-23-161Z.log` still fails on the unrelated `flow stop during a looped flow prevents later iterations from continuing` case before any Task 69 proof owner is named, with `ownershipRelease.branch === "never_reached"` and 20 retained stop-unwind checkpoints ending at repeated `runFlowInstruction.returnResult`. By contrast, Task 66's retained prerequisite proof `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-10T05-12-00-126Z.log` shows the same test as `ok 279 - flow stop during a looped flow prevents later iterations from continuing`, so the new contradiction is a reopened full-suite prerequisite, not missing repo-list implementation or proof authoring inside Task 69. External-library and framework confirmation point to the same diagnosis boundary rather than a timing workaround: Context7 `/nodejs/node`, DeepWiki `nodejs/node`, and the official Node docs all confirm that `EventEmitter.emit()` invokes listeners synchronously (`https://nodejs.org/api/events.html`), the `node:test` runner can report extraneous asynchronous activity after a test function has already finished instead of delaying all reporting (`https://nodejs.org/api/test.html#extraneous-asynchronous-activity`), and `process.getActiveResourcesInfo()` is the supported way to inspect resources that still keep the event loop alive once the real owner seam has been isolated (`https://nodejs.org/api/process.html#processgetactiveresourcesinfo`). Issue-resolution references from engineers in practice line up with that same fix shape: synchronous `emit()` does not magically prove later async caller cleanup, and test hangs/open-handle failures are resolved by fixing the owning teardown/resource seam rather than adding waits (`https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50294746/is-eventemitter-code-blocking-or-not`, `https://stackoverflow.com/questions/53266915/why-doesnt-jest-complete-the-async-operations-in-this-node-test`). The chosen solution is therefore to stop treating Task 69 as the active owner, split or reorder the plan again, and hand execution back to one bounded prerequisite task in the `flows.run.loop` runtime chain until the full `server:unit` baseline is trustworthy again. That fits the current repo state because Task 69's scoped files and direct proof homes are already implemented on disk, while the blocking full-wrapper failure never enters those owners and instead reproduces in the unrelated flow-runtime test before the repo-list assertions can matter. Rejected alternatives are not suitable: continuing Task 69 implementation or browser/manual proof would be dishonest because those owners are not reached; widening repo-list changes around another `flows.run.loop` regression would break the repo's existing owner-handoff pattern; and timeout growth, wrapper changes, or terminate-first websocket cleanup are unsupported workarounds that would mask a prerequisite cleanup defect instead of proving and fixing its real owner.

- **RESOLVED ISSUE** 2026-04-10 Testing 5 (`npm run test:summary:client`) stopped on two unrelated client timeouts outside this shared repo-list/read-model scope. What I tried: fixed the in-scope `useIngestRoots` waiting-overlay lock normalization in `client/src/hooks/useIngestRoots.ts`, reran the targeted wrapper `npm run test:summary:client -- --file client/src/test/useIngestRoots.test.tsx` to confirm the task-owned proof returned to green (`tests run: 7`, `passed: 7`, log `test-results/client-tests-2026-04-10T18-50-37-404Z.log`), then reran the full client wrapper. Exact contradiction: the full wrapper still failed at `test-results/client-tests-2026-04-10T18-50-45-299Z.log`, but the remaining failing suites were `src/test/chatPage.codexDefaults.test.tsx` and `src/test/chatPage.flags.sandbox.payload.test.tsx`, both timing out after 5000 ms while this task's owned `src/test/useIngestRoots.test.tsx` and `src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx` both passed. Planner repair on 2026-04-10 resolved the task-shape defect by moving the active owner to new Task 69 instead of widening this repo-list task into unrelated chat-page timeout diagnosis.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** Fresh blocker research on 2026-04-10 proves the correct technical answer is planner repair that hands the current blocker to one new bounded client-chat prerequisite task instead of widening this shared repo-list task or raising timeouts. Repository precedents first: `code_info` found the same owner-handoff pattern in this repo when broad wrappers failed outside the current task's documented proof owners, including Task 3 -> Task 4, Task 11 -> Tasks 12 through 15, Task 33 -> Task 34 plus its follow-on chain, and this story's own Task 66/67/68 -> Task 69 handoffs in `planning/0000055-users-can-queue-ingest-and-re-embed-requests.md`; in each case the plan split or re-owned the blocker rather than stretching the current task across unrelated suites. Current local evidence matches that pattern exactly. The targeted shared repo-list client proof `test-results/client-tests-2026-04-10T18-50-37-404Z.log` shows `PASS src/test/useIngestRoots.test.tsx`, and the full client wrapper `test-results/client-tests-2026-04-10T18-50-45-299Z.log` shows both shared repo-list-owned suites green (`PASS src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx`, `PASS src/test/useIngestRoots.test.tsx`) while the only remaining failures are `FAIL src/test/chatPage.codexDefaults.test.tsx` and `FAIL src/test/chatPage.flags.sandbox.payload.test.tsx` with `Exceeded timeout of 5000 ms for a test`. `code_info` also confirmed those failing suites belong to the chat defaults/payload path around `client/src/pages/ChatPage.tsx`, `client/src/hooks/useChatModel.ts`, `client/src/hooks/useChatStream.ts`, and `client/src/components/chat/CodexFlagsPanel.tsx`, and do not overlap this task's repo-list/read-model owners in `client/src/hooks/useIngestRoots.ts`, `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx`, `client/src/components/ingest/RootDetailsDrawer.tsx`, or `server/src/lmstudio/toolService.ts`. External-library and framework confirmation point to the same fix shape, not a timeout workaround: official Jest docs say `test(name, fn, timeout)` uses a default 5000 ms timeout and `jest.setTimeout()` only changes the timeout budget for genuinely long-running tests (`https://jestjs.io/docs/api`, `https://jestjs.io/docs/jest-object`); Testing Library docs say async helpers already retry with their own bounded timeouts and require waiting on the actual async condition rather than sleeping past it (`https://testing-library.com/docs/dom-testing-library/api-async`, `https://testing-library.com/docs/dom-testing-library/api-configuration`); DeepWiki's Jest summary says per-test timeout increases are supported but still secondary to fixing the owning async behavior. Issue-resolution references from engineers in practice line up with that same conclusion: timeout errors commonly mean an unresolved async owner or wrong awaited condition, not that an unrelated feature task should absorb the failing suite (`https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49603939/message-async-callback-was-not-invoked-within-the-5000-ms-timeout-specified-by`, `https://stackoverflow.com/questions/70766349/why-is-my-test-failing-on-an-expectation-wrapped-in-waitfor`). The chosen solution therefore fits the current repo state because this task's documented owners and direct proof homes are already implemented and green, while the blocker now lives entirely in different chat-page suites and source files. Rejected alternatives are not suitable: continuing this task toward e2e/manual proof would be dishonest while the full client wrapper is red; widening this task to include chat defaults/payload logic would break the repo's established owner-boundary pattern; and bumping global or per-file timeouts would be an unsupported workaround that masks the real chat async owner instead of proving and fixing it.

This task returns to `__to_do__` with its completed implementation subtasks plus Testing 1 through 4 preserved; Testing 5 and 6 remain the next proof gates after Task 69 restores or cleanly re-hands off the full client baseline.

### Task 71. Restore Queue Duplicate Diagnostics And Blocking Waiter Error Semantics

- Repository Name: `Current Repository`
- Task Dependencies: `70`
- Task Status: `__done__`
- Notes: Added on 2026-04-09 from review pass `0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922` after the findings artifact endorsed one duplicate-diagnostic contract regression and one blocking-waiter error-mapping regression.

#### Overview

This task repairs the queue admission and blocking-wait surfaces that currently hide important queue state. Waiting duplicate updates must log that queued work was updated rather than duplicated, and queue-state read failures during blocking waits must surface as queue-read failures instead of masquerading as ordinary wait timeouts. The fix should stay bounded to queue diagnostics and blocking-wait error semantics; it should not broaden into unrelated queue admission redesign or transport-contract changes outside the repaired error mapping.

#### Task Exit Criteria

- Waiting duplicate updates keep the existing queue success contract while also producing a distinct operator-visible diagnostic that the queued request was updated in place rather than duplicated.
- The route and service layers preserve the helper's duplicate-reuse diagnostics instead of dropping them on the floor.
- Queue-state read failures in the blocking waiter no longer settle as ordinary `timeout` results.
- `reingestService` maps blocking queue-read failures to a distinct queue-read or backend-read error path instead of `WAIT_TIMEOUT`.
- Blocking-wait setup, timeout, queue-read rejection, and other early-return exits unregister the shared waiter/listener state before returning so later callers do not inherit leaked queue-aware waiters.
- Direct proof exists for both the duplicate-diagnostic path and the queue-read-failure path in the route, service, and one transport-mirror proof that consumes the same blocking result.
- This task stays bounded to the endorsed queue-diagnostic and waiter-error findings from this review pass.

#### Documentation Locations

- `planning/0000055-users-can-queue-ingest-and-re-embed-requests.md`
- `planning/0000055-pr-summary.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-evidence.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-findings.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-blind-spot-challenge.md`
- `server/src/ingest/requestQueue.ts`
- `server/src/routes/ingestStart.ts`
- `server/src/routes/ingestReembed.ts`
- `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts`
- `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/ingest-start.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/ingest-reembed.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/reingestService.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/integration/commands.reingest.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/features/ingest-start.feature`
- `server/src/test/features/ingest-reembed.feature`

#### Proof Mapping

- Start-ingest duplicate-update diagnostic: owned by `server/src/ingest/requestQueue.ts` and `server/src/routes/ingestStart.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-start.test.ts`.
- Re-embed duplicate-update diagnostic: owned by `server/src/ingest/requestQueue.ts` and `server/src/routes/ingestReembed.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-reembed.test.ts`.
- Queue-read failure stays distinct from timeout in the blocking waiter: owned by `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts` and `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/reingestService.test.ts`.
- Queue-aware waiter cleanup stays listener-safe for queue-read rejection, genuine timeout, and terminal early-return exits: owned by `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts` and `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/reingestService.test.ts`.
- Command-facing blocking caller keeps the repaired queue-read failure semantics: owned by `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/integration/commands.reingest.test.ts`.
- The supported start and re-embed feature paths still accept queued work through the normal Testcontainers-backed API surface after the diagnostic repair: owned by `server/src/routes/ingestStart.ts` and `server/src/routes/ingestReembed.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/features/ingest-start.feature` and `server/src/test/features/ingest-reembed.feature`.

### Task 73. Reuse Admission Validation For Deferred Queue Execution And The Deletions-Only Re-Embed Fast Path

- Repository Name: `Current Repository`
- Task Dependencies: `72`
- Task Status: `__done__`
- Notes: Added on 2026-04-09 from review pass `0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922` after the findings artifact endorsed one deferred-execution validation gap and one deletions-only re-embed fast-path regression. Re-owned to `__to_do__` on 2026-04-10 by planner repair after the first full `server:unit` rerun for this task reproved an unrelated `flows.run.loop` prerequisite before the remaining Task 73 proof gates could finish honestly, then promoted back to `__in_progress__` on 2026-04-10 after Task 72's bounded runtime repair proved the full wrapper now fails only in this task's own malformed-payload proof homes.

#### Overview

This task repairs two server runtime seams where deferred execution no longer respects the intended admission contract. First, queued execution must reject malformed queued payloads at execution time instead of defaulting them into later provider failures. Second, the deletions-only re-embed fast path must keep the same zero-work bootstrap-failure tolerance as the already-hardened no-embedding-work branch, so a run that has only deletion cleanup left does not fail on unnecessary Chroma bootstrap work. The fix should stay bounded to those endorsed runtime seams and their proof surfaces.

#### Task Exit Criteria

- Deferred queued execution reuses the admission-time required-model rule, or an equivalent shared validator, before execution proceeds.
- Malformed queued payloads fail as explicit execution-time validation failures instead of degrading into later provider or network failures.
- Both queue promotion and startup recovery hit the repaired validation boundary before provider work begins when the queued payload is malformed.
- Malformed queued payload validation failures finalize through the owned queue error or cleanup path and release queue ownership without leaving partial running state behind.
- Deletions-only re-embed runs remain successful when there is no embedding work left and Chroma bootstrap metadata cannot be fetched.
- The repaired runtime behavior has direct proof in the server unit suites that own queue runtime and re-embed fast-path behavior.
- This task stays bounded to the endorsed deferred-validation and deletions-only fast-path findings from this review pass.

#### Documentation Locations

- `planning/0000055-users-can-queue-ingest-and-re-embed-requests.md`
- `planning/0000055-pr-summary.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-evidence.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-findings.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-blind-spot-challenge.md`
- `server/src/ingest/requestContracts.ts`
- `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/ingest-queue-runtime.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/ingest-reembed.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/features/ingest-delta-reembed.feature`

#### Proof Mapping

- Deferred queued execution rejects malformed payloads during queue promotion and finalizes the rejected item without leaving active queue ownership behind: owned by `server/src/ingest/requestContracts.ts` and `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-queue-runtime.test.ts`.
- Deferred queued execution rejects malformed payloads during startup recovery and finalizes the rejected item without leaving partial running state behind: owned by `server/src/ingest/requestContracts.ts` and `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-queue-runtime.test.ts`.
- Malformed queued payloads fail at validation rather than later provider work: owned by `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-queue-runtime.test.ts`.
- Deletions-only re-embed survives Chroma bootstrap failure when no embedding work remains: owned by `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-reembed.test.ts`.
- The supported delta re-embed feature path still completes deletions-only cleanup after the fast-path repair on the repository’s Testcontainers-backed integration harness: owned by `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/features/ingest-delta-reembed.feature`.

I ran the exact predecessor wrapper from current disk after commit `198c5356`, and Docker failed to fetch anonymous auth metadata for `node:22-slim` / `docker/dockerfile:1.7` with `lookup auth.docker.io on 192.168.65.7:53: read udp ... i/o timeout`, which is outside the bounded client install retry seam this task owns. Planner repair on 2026-04-11 resolved this live blocker by moving the active blocked state into new Task 75, which now owns Docker registry reachability before this repo-owned compose-build handoff can continue honestly.

Repository precedents first: `AGENTS.md` says likely transient Docker/Compose network or cache failures get one honest retry before deeper investigation, and this task already used its bounded repo-owned repair by adding supported npm fetch-retry and timeout settings plus the existing `CODEINFO_NPM_REGISTRY` seam in `client/Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.e2e.yml`. Story 55 also repeatedly re-owns blockers when the red wrapper path moves outside the active task seam instead of widening unrelated implementation work.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** External-library precedents found: official Docker docs route this failure class to Docker environment configuration, not application-layer Dockerfile edits. Docker's daemon troubleshooting guide says registry reachability failures caused by DNS resolution should be handled by setting Docker daemon DNS servers in `/etc/docker/daemon.json` or by disabling `dnsmasq` so Docker can reach public nameservers; the `dockerd` reference documents the supported `--dns` daemon option; the daemon proxy docs say registry access should use Docker daemon proxy settings or Desktop proxy settings; the Docker Desktop networking docs say Desktop DNS-resolution behavior and proxy settings are the supported controls there; `docker login` is the supported authentication path for registry access; and the BuildKit docs say registry mirrors belong in BuildKit configuration rather than in application build commands. Context7 confirmed the same supported BuildKit mirror pattern for `docker.io`, and DeepWiki's `docker/docs` summary matched the same official mitigation set: daemon/Desktop DNS, proxy configuration, registry mirrors, and authenticated pulls.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** Issue-resolution references found: the exact error class in the blocker note, `failed to fetch anonymous auth metadata` / `lookup auth.docker.io ... i/o timeout`, is a known Docker/BuildKit registry-network failure that occurs before later Dockerfile layers or browser proof homes run. Current disk still retains the earlier `logs/test-summaries/e2e-tests-latest.log` wrapper failure in the client-image `npm ci` lane, while the live blocker note records the later post-`198c5356` rerun that moved to Docker image metadata resolution; both red paths are still external registry/network failures before Task 77 browser execution. Public issue reports line up with the same class: Docker Desktop users report `auth.docker.io` and `registry-1.docker.io` handshake or token timeouts during pulls/builds, `moby/buildkit` reports the same `failed to fetch oauth token` metadata failure for `docker.io` images, and long-standing Docker timeout reports tie `lookup auth.docker.io ... i/o timeout` to Docker DNS/network configuration rather than application code regressions.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** Chosen fix and why it fits current local repo state: do not add more repo code changes under Task 76 unless a later rerun proves the failure has moved back into the bounded client-image install seam. The honest next action is to rerun `npm run compose:e2e:build` or the targeted `npm run test:summary:e2e` unchanged when external connectivity is healthy; if the same `auth.docker.io` / Docker Hub metadata timeout recurs, repair the Docker environment outside this repo-owned seam by configuring supported daemon or Desktop DNS/proxy settings, authenticating to Docker Hub with `docker login`, or using a Docker/BuildKit registry mirror. That fits current local state because the allowed repo files can influence npm install reliability inside the client build layer, but they do not control Docker daemon or Desktop registry-auth metadata resolution for `node:22-slim` or `docker/dockerfile:1.7`.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** Rejected alternatives are not suitable: more npm fetch-tuning in `client/Dockerfile` does not fix a failure that happens before the relevant Dockerfile layer executes; more `RootsTable`, Jest, Playwright, or screenshot work would be dishonest because the wrapper never reaches browser execution; ad hoc sleeps or unsupported retry loops in repo code would hide an environment-level owner instead of using Docker's supported DNS/proxy/login/mirror surfaces; and marking Testing 1 or Testing 2 complete would be false while the wrapper is still blocked before Task 77's proof seam.
- Planner repair on 2026-04-11 narrowed this task to the repo-owned compose-build handoff that resumes after Task 75 proves Docker registry reachability. This task now waits as `__to_do__` instead of remaining the highest active blocked owner.

Closed Task 76 as `__done__`; Task 77 is now the next owner for the remaining browser-proof close-out.

### Task 77. Align `RootsTable` Bulk Re-Embed Affordances And Batch Refresh Behavior

- Repository Name: `Current Repository`
- Task Dependencies: `76`
- Task Status: `__done__`
- Notes: Added on 2026-04-09 from review pass `0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922` after the findings artifact endorsed one bulk re-embed affordance mismatch and one batch-refresh churn regression in `RootsTable`. Re-owned to `__to_do__` on 2026-04-10 by planner repair after the repeated setup-only e2e compose-build `npm ci` blocker proved a separate prerequisite owner must restore browser setup reliability before this task's remaining browser-proof gate can finish honestly. Planner repair on 2026-04-11 kept this task waiting while new Task 75 now owns Docker registry reachability and Task 76 now owns the resumed compose-build handoff after that external prerequisite lands.

#### Overview

This task repairs the changed client bulk flow so shared selection, row-level re-embed affordances, and bulk payload construction obey one explicit invariant. It also removes the per-success refresh churn from batch-shaped actions so roots and model refreshes happen at the batch boundary rather than once per successful row. The fix must stay bounded to `RootsTable` behavior, its direct tests, and any one screenshot or browser proof needed to keep the repaired batch contract visible.

#### Task Exit Criteria

- Rows are not silently excluded from `Re-embed selected` while remaining individually re-embeddable under the same visible state.
- Shared selection, bulk re-embed targeting, and row-level re-embed button gating use one explicit invariant, whether by broadening shared selection or by narrowing the row-level affordance to match.
- If a row becomes non-bulk-reembeddable after it was previously selected, that row is cleared from shared selection as soon as the live row state changes and the submit path re-filters against the same live eligibility set so stale local selection cannot leak into the outgoing batch payload.
- The repaired bulk submit path reuses the current loaded row state and one batch request rather than triggering a pre-submit reload or one eligibility probe per selected row.
- Batch-shaped re-embed and remove flows refresh roots and models at the batch boundary instead of once per successful row, unless direct proof shows one narrower exception is strictly required.
- Direct proof exists in `client/src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx` for the repaired shared-selection contract and the repaired refresh call count.
- If the repaired affordance changes a retained visible browser seam, one explicit browser proof and screenshot asset exist for that repaired seam.
- If any intentional asymmetry remains between row-level re-embed and bulk re-embed, this task's final notes name the exact product reason and visible signpost.
- This task stays bounded to the endorsed `RootsTable` affordance and refresh findings from this review pass.

#### Documentation Locations

- `planning/0000055-users-can-queue-ingest-and-re-embed-requests.md`
- `planning/0000055-pr-summary.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-evidence.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-findings.md`
- `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx`
- `client/src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx`
- `e2e/ingest.spec.ts`
- `artifacts/story-0000055-screenshots/0000055-bulk-selection-state.png`

#### Proof Mapping

- Shared selection and row-level re-embed affordances stay aligned for the repaired row state: owned by `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx`; prove in `client/src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx`.
- Selected-row stale-state handling stays explicit when a row becomes ineligible before submit: owned by `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx`; prove in `client/src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx` and, if the visible seam changes, `e2e/ingest.spec.ts`.
- Bulk re-embed submission stays bounded to the current loaded row set and one batch request: owned by `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx`; prove in `client/src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx`.
- Batch refresh and model reload happen once per batch action: owned by `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx`; prove in `client/src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx`.
- Any retained visible browser seam stays honest after the repair: owned by `e2e/ingest.spec.ts` and `artifacts/story-0000055-screenshots/0000055-bulk-selection-state.png`; prove with the full `npm run test:summary:e2e` wrapper output and the updated screenshot artifact when that seam changes.

#### Implementation notes

- Inserted on 2026-04-09 from review pass `0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922` because the stored findings artifact showed that `RootsTable` now applies one selection rule to bulk re-embed and a different affordance rule to row-level re-embed, and it still multiplies refresh side effects once per successful row even though the user-visible flow is batch-shaped.
- 2026-04-10 reread and inspection checkpoint: re-read `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-evidence.md` and `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260409T201302Z-13774922-findings.md`, then inspected `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx`, `client/src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx`, and the current browser proof in `e2e/ingest.spec.ts`. Current disk still matches the finding: the row-level `Re-embed` button ignores `blocksSharedSelection(...)`, bulk submit still trusts stale `selected` state for re-embed targets, and both bulk re-embed and bulk remove still call refresh hooks once per successful row.
- 2026-04-10 implementation checkpoint: updated `client/src/components/ingest/RootsTable.tsx` so the row-level `Re-embed` button now uses the same `blocksSharedSelection(...)` bulk-eligibility rule as the shared checkbox model, the bulk re-embed submit path re-filters against the current live eligible set at click time, and row-level versus bulk re-embed no longer diverge for waiting, running, cleanup-blocked, or active-head rows.
- 2026-04-10 batch-boundary checkpoint: moved `onRefresh()` and `onRefreshModels?.()` to the batch-success boundary inside `handleBulk(...)`, while keeping the single-row handlers on their existing per-action refresh path. That closes the per-success refresh churn without widening into unrelated message or table-layout changes.
- 2026-04-10 proof-authoring checkpoint: updated `client/src/test/ingestRoots.test.tsx` with distinct re-embed-affordance, stale-selection-clear, live-eligibility re-filter, and batch-refresh-cardinality cases, and rewrote the affected titles so they no longer pretend remove-focused selection tests also prove row-versus-bulk re-embed alignment. Updated `e2e/ingest.spec.ts` so the retained screenshot `artifacts/story-0000055-screenshots/0000055-bulk-selection-state.png` is now owned by a deterministic `aligned row and bulk re-embed affordances` browser scenario rather than the older remove-focused flow.
- 2026-04-10 automated proof checkpoint: `npm run build:summary:client` passed cleanly with `warning_count: 0`, `agent_action: skip_log`, and log `logs/test-summaries/build-client-latest.log`, so Testing 1 is honestly complete before the client and browser wrappers run.
- 2026-04-10 automated proof checkpoint: `npm run test:summary:client` passed cleanly with `tests run: 680`, `passed: 680`, `failed: 0`, `agent_action: skip_log`, and log `test-results/client-tests-2026-04-10T23-38-30-473Z.log`, so Testing 2 is honestly complete and the full client baseline now carries the repaired `RootsTable` affordance and refresh proofs.
- 2026-04-11 automated proof checkpoint: `npm run test:summary:e2e` passed cleanly with `tests run: 49`, `passed: 49`, `failed: 0`, `agent_action: skip_log`, and log `logs/test-summaries/e2e-tests-latest.log`. Testing 3 is honestly complete, and the full browser wrapper now carries the repaired `aligned row and bulk re-embed affordances` scenario and retained screenshot seam on current disk.
- **RESOLVED ISSUE** 2026-04-10 Testing 3 `npm run test:summary:e2e`: the wrapper failed twice during setup before any browser assertions ran because the e2e compose build hit a transient external `npm ci` `ECONNRESET` while building the client image in Docker (`logs/test-summaries/e2e-tests-latest.log`). I tried the one allowed honest retry of the same wrapper after inspecting the first setup-failure log, and the second run failed in the same compose-build step with the same network-aborted client-image install, so there was no task-owned browser or Playwright seam to repair from current repo code. Planner repair on 2026-04-10 resolved this blocker by moving the active owner to new Task 76 so the compose-build reliability issue could be handled before this task's remaining browser-proof gate resumes.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** Fresh blocker research on 2026-04-10 proves the correct technical answer is not more current-task `RootsTable`, Jest, Playwright, or screenshot work. Repository precedents first: `AGENTS.md` explicitly says likely transient Docker/Compose network or cache build failures get one honest retry before deeper investigation, and the retry already happened here; Story 55 also repeatedly re-owns blockers when the red wrapper path is outside the active task's source seam. Current local evidence matches that pattern: `logs/test-summaries/e2e-tests-latest.log` shows the failure occurs during `npm run compose:e2e:build`, specifically `client/Dockerfile` step `RUN npm ci --workspaces --include-workspace-root --ignore-scripts --no-audit --no-fund`, and the wrapper never reaches `e2e:test`. The same Dockerfile already exposes the bounded environment seam `CODEINFO_NPM_REGISTRY`, so the task-owned code is not the failing layer. External-library and framework confirmation support an environment/build-owner fix rather than more UI churn: official npm config docs say npm already retries idempotent registry reads on network failures and exposes supported knobs `fetch-retries`, `fetch-retry-factor`, `fetch-retry-mintimeout`, `fetch-retry-maxtimeout`, `fetch-timeout`, plus `proxy` and `https-proxy` / matching environment variables for unstable or proxied networks. DeepWiki on `npm/cli` did not surface more specific guidance for this case, and Context7 library resolution failed with a transport fetch error during this step, so the external proof here relies on the official npm docs plus targeted issue-resolution references. Those issue-resolution references line up with the same conclusion: GitHub `actions/runner-images` issue `#3737` documents intermittent `npm ci` `ECONNRESET` failures in CI as external connectivity incidents, and Docker/NPM practitioners on Stack Overflow report the same class of Docker-build install failure as registry/proxy/cache/network configuration trouble rather than app-code regressions. The chosen fix fits the current repo state because the only failing command is the Docker build's client-image dependency install, the repo already has a documented retry-once rule and an existing registry-override seam, and no browser assertion or repaired Task 77 client behavior has failed yet. Rejected alternatives are not suitable: widening Task 77 into more `RootsTable` edits or Playwright rewrites would be dishonest because the wrapper never reaches browser execution; marking Testing 3 complete would be false; and inventing unsupported sleeps, screenshot-only work, or ad hoc npm-flag churn inside the UI task would hide an external compose-build owner instead of naming it cleanly. If the network stabilizes, rerun `npm run test:summary:e2e` unchanged; if the same setup-only `npm ci` `ECONNRESET` keeps recurring, the next honest owner is a separate compose-build reliability repair that uses the existing registry/proxy seam or supported npm fetch-retry configuration rather than more Task 77 product-code changes.
- 2026-04-10 planner repair first moved the active owner to new Task 76 because the repeated e2e setup failure still lived in compose-build reliability before browser execution started. Planner repair on 2026-04-11 then kept this task waiting while Task 75 took over Docker registry reachability and Task 76 narrowed to the resumed repo-owned compose-build handoff.
- 2026-04-11 implementation-only audit: re-read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and this exact Task 77 section from disk after Task 76 closed, confirmed Subtasks 1 through 17 plus Testing 1 and 2 already honestly match current repo evidence, and verified that the earlier setup-only e2e blocker is now only historical after Tasks 75 and 76. Promoted Task 77 to `__in_progress__` as the next active owner; only Testing 3 remains open before the later proof-completion audit can close this task honestly.
- 2026-04-11 implementation-plus-automated-proof audit: re-read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and this exact Task 77 section from disk after commit `4978133c`, confirmed Testing 3 now honestly matches current repo evidence alongside the earlier completed subtasks and client proofs, and verified that no live `**BLOCKER**` note remains on this task. Closed Task 77 as `__done__`; Task 78 is now the next owner for final revalidation.

## Code Review Findings

- Review handoff: `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-current-review.json`

- Disposition: reopen Story 55 for one `must_fix`, six `should_fix`, and one localized `optional_simplification` finding in `Current Repository`.
- Required fix summary:
  - Treat `cleanup-blocked` queue rows as live duplicate owners so repo-list overlays cannot hide a stalled blocked row behind a later waiting request.
  - Restrict destructive or re-embed follow-up surfaces to the roots they are actually allowed to target, including the e2e cleanup route's caller-supplied `:root` selector and queued start-ingest rows that were never ingested.
  - Keep queue admission, deferred queue execution, and shared repo-list normalization on one stable contract across server and client instead of silently rewriting malformed inputs or dropping normalized error codes.
  - Restore the unrelated vendored Bats fixture semantics that were widened by this branch, replace the fixed one-second cancel sleep with deterministic proof boundaries, and finish the localized schema-version constant cleanup while this review-created repair block is active.
- Challenge outcome: no additional findings were generated, but the blind-spot challenge strengthened the same queue-admission, repo-list overlay, cleanup-route, and proof-quality concerns recorded in the findings artifact. Residual weak proof remains around the timeout-fallback path in `waitForQueueRequestTerminalStatus()`, active overlay display-field precedence, and browser timing sensitivity until the reopened tasks land and the fresh revalidation task reruns the shared wrappers.

Repository precedents first: this repo already handles the same failure class by splitting or re-owning work once a bounded wrapper lane exhausts cleanly, including Task 4's wrapper-baseline split, Task 62's false-green successor handoff, Task 64's caller-boundary re-own, and Task 80's earlier `**BLOCKING ANSWER**` that moved unrelated full-wrapper work out of a product-code task instead of widening it. Current local evidence matches that pattern again. The old Task 79 lane is green in isolation: `npm run test:summary:server:unit -- --file server/src/test/integration/flows.run.loop.test.ts --test-name "flow stop during a looped flow prevents later iterations from continuing"` passed cleanly (`test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-11T13-32-15-319Z.log`), and the later file where the full wrapper last reports progress also passes cleanly in isolation: `npm run test:summary:server:unit -- --file server/src/test/unit/flows.flags.test.ts` finished with `tests run: 7`, `passed: 7`, `failed: 0`, `agent_action: skip_log`, and retained log `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-11T14-04-46-435Z.log`. External-library and framework confirmation point to the same ownership boundary: Context7 `/nodejs/node`, DeepWiki `nodejs/node`, and the official Node docs show that the wrapper can only emit a clean final verdict after the spawned child reaches `close`, and that `close` happens only after the child exits and stdio closes; `node:test` also documents extraneous asynchronous activity after a test ends instead of silently waiting for it, and `process.getActiveResourcesInfo()` is the supported way to inspect resource types that still keep the event loop alive. Issue-resolution references in practice line up with that same fix shape: engineers resolve this kind of Node non-exit by finding the still-ref'ed timer, handle, or listener and closing or `unref()`-ing the real owner, not by changing the wrapper or forcing process termination (see `https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47312280/why-node-js-doesnt-exit-after-promise-call` and `https://stackoverflow.com/questions/18050095/node-js-will-node-always-wait-for-settimeout-to-complete-before-exiting`). The chosen fix fits the current repo state because both the old loop file and the later `flows.flags` file now close cleanly when isolated, while the full wrapper still fails to reach child `close` after the later file; that means the honest next work is one bounded successor task for the suite-only post-`flows.flags` non-terminal boundary, centered on `server/src/test/unit/flows.flags.test.ts`, `server/src/mongo/repo.ts`, `server/src/mongo/events.ts`, and at most one directly shared helper if a fresh rerun still points there. Rejected alternatives are not suitable: do not keep widening Task 79's `flows.run.loop` scope after it exhausted cleanly; do not change `scripts/test-summary-server-unit.mjs` or `scripts/summary-wrapper-protocol.mjs` because those files only report whether the child reached `close`; do not mark Testing 2 complete from isolated green proofs alone; and do not use `process.exit()`, `--forceExit`, arbitrary sleeps, or larger timeout budgets because those would hide the real lingering-resource owner instead of naming and repairing it.

External/library precedents confirm the same boundary. Context7 `/nodejs/node` and DeepWiki `nodejs/node` both say `node:test` reports extraneous async activity instead of waiting for it to settle, `child_process` `close` happens only after the child exits and stdio closes, `process.getActiveResourcesInfo()` is the supported way to identify resources still keeping the event loop alive, and `Timeout` handles keep the event loop alive unless cleared or `unref()`'d. The local wrapper implementation in `scripts/summary-wrapper-protocol.mjs` matches that exactly: it only finishes on child `close`, and a watchdog can report `terminal_summary_without_close` or `semantic_progress_stalled`, but it does not invent a synthetic pass state. Issue-resolution references in practice point to the same fix shape: `https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47312280/why-node-js-doesnt-exit-after-promise-call` and `https://stackoverflow.com/questions/18050095/node-js-will-node-always-wait-for-settimeout-to-complete-before-exiting` both explain that ref'ed timers or other active handles keep Node alive, so the correct repair is to fix the real child-side owner rather than the supervising wrapper. Chosen fix: do not keep debugging Task 80's post-`flows.flags` files, and do not modify `scripts/test-summary-server-unit.mjs` or `scripts/summary-wrapper-protocol.mjs`; instead, repair the plan so a fresh prerequisite task owns the current earlier `flows.run.loop` contradiction across `server/src/test/integration/flows.run.loop.test.ts`, `server/src/flows/service.ts`, `server/src/agents/runLock.ts`, and `server/src/chat/inflightRegistry.ts`, then resume Task 80 only after that prerequisite restores or re-hands off the full-wrapper baseline honestly. This fits the current repo state because the later file is already green in isolation, while the full wrapper now fails before reaching that boundary at all. Rejected alternatives are not suitable: continuing Task 80 implementation would be dishonest because the blocker no longer overlaps its proof seam, changing the wrapper would only hide the child-side owner, and `process.exit()`, `--forceExit`, arbitrary sleeps, or broader timeout budgets would mask the cleanup-ordering defect instead of naming and repairing it.

  External-library and framework precedents confirm that this is the correct boundary and that the repair now sits in the right place. The official `ws` server examples keep message-routing ownership at ingress inside `wss.on('connection')` and `ws.on('message')`, which matches why the landed fix belongs in `server/src/ws/server.ts` instead of another later `flows.run.loop` cleanup edit. Official Node documentation, confirmed through Context7 `/nodejs/node` and DeepWiki `nodejs/node`, says `node:test` reports asynchronous activity that outlives the test instead of hiding it, child-process `'close'` is the post-stdio signal rather than `'exit'`, and `process.getActiveResourcesInfo()` is the supported way to identify event-loop resources still keeping a child test process alive. Those sources support the same conclusion the repo now proves: the wrapper was correctly surfacing the missing pending-cancel handoff, and the right fix was at the earlier runtime owner rather than in wrapper timing or a later cleanup hook. I attempted the required `code_info` lookup first for repo-pattern research, but the MCP method is unavailable in this environment, so the repository-precedent portion of this proof comes from direct current-disk evidence plus the landed Task 84 commit and retained logs.

  Exact issue-resolution references in practice also line up with the now-landed fix rather than with any workaround. Official Node docs and common engineering guidance both treat `close`-after-stdio and active-resource inspection as the honest way to diagnose async cleanup boundaries, not as a reason to add force-exit behavior, sleeps, or wider timeouts. Chosen fix: accept the current repo evidence that the prerequisite fix has already landed, keep Task 85 bounded to its own `flows.run.loop` cleanup owner files, and resume Task 85's targeted-versus-full rerun sequence from current `HEAD` instead of creating another earlier-owner task. ### Task 87. Preserve Cleanup-Blocked Queue Ownership And Repo-List Overlay Integrity

- Repository Name: `Current Repository`
- Task Dependencies: `86`
- Task Status: `__done__`
- Notes: Added after review pass `0000055-20260411T104227Z-756a77d1` found that `cleanup-blocked` queue rows are neither treated as live duplicates nor protected from being overwritten by a later waiting overlay for the same canonical target. Re-owned to `__to_do__` on 2026-04-11 by planner repair after the current earlier-lane prerequisite again proved a fresh `flows.run.loop` owner must land before this task's remaining wrapper proof can continue honestly. Re-owned to `__to_do__` again on 2026-04-11 after the newest full-wrapper rerun proved the remaining red path still belongs to a fresh earlier `flows.run.loop` prerequisite rather than to another local queue-owner change.

#### Overview

This review-fix task repairs the queue admission and repo-list overlay contract around `cleanup-blocked` rows. Story 55 promised that a blocked cleanup state stays visible and continues to stall progress until the underlying delete problem is resolved; the current admission and overlay code lets a later waiting request for the same target hide that stalled state instead.

#### Task Exit Criteria

- Queue admission treats `cleanup-blocked` rows as live duplicate owners for the same canonical target instead of creating a second request that can hide the blocked row.
- The live-target uniqueness rule or equivalent live-owner selector used by the queue model treats `waiting`, `running`, and `cleanup-blocked` as one canonical-target ownership set wherever the story contract requires a single visible owner for one root.
- Cleanup-blocked rows remain visible to both queue writers and repo-list readers, and they continue to stall newer waiting work until the cleanup owner clears the blocker instead of being implicitly superseded by later submissions.
- `/ingest/roots` and the shared repo-list builder preserve the blocked row's visible error state, request identity, and queue ownership instead of letting a later waiting overlay replace it.
- Direct server tests prove that a cleanup-blocked row remains the visible owner and duplicate admission target until the cleanup blocker is cleared.

- Cleanup ownership and blocked-row stall behavior stay consistent across writer and runtime-reader surfaces until the blocker is cleared: owned by `server/src/ingest/requestQueue.ts` plus queue runtime cleanup flow; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-queue-runtime.test.ts`.
- Repo-list overlay precedence for a blocked row versus a later waiting row on the same root: owned by `server/src/lmstudio/toolService.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/tools-ingested-repos.test.ts`.
- `/ingest/roots` blocked-owner visibility after the overlay repair: owned by the repo-list route surface and shared list builder; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-roots-dedupe.test.ts`.

- Inserted on 2026-04-11 from review pass `0000055-20260411T104227Z-756a77d1` to repair the endorsed cleanup-blocked duplicate-admission and blocked-row overlay defect before the story can stay closed honestly.

Current disk still excludes `cleanup-blocked` from the duplicate-owner and partial-unique-index path, and the queue overlay loop still applies later waiting rows after earlier blocked rows in simple creation order, so the same canonical target can be hidden by a fresher waiting overlay unless all three seams are repaired together.
- 2026-04-11 implementation: `server/src/ingest/requestQueue.ts` now treats `cleanup-blocked` rows as live duplicate owners through one shared live-target lookup instead of only reusing `waiting` and `running`, and the returned queue result now preserves the true `queueState` of any reused owner instead of flattening every non-running row to `waiting`.
- 2026-04-11 implementation: `server/src/mongo/ingestQueueRequest.ts` now keeps `cleanup-blocked` inside the `ingest_queue_live_target_unique_idx` partial unique index so persisted live-owner enforcement matches the in-memory duplicate-owner rule instead of allowing a second live row for the same canonical target during blocked cleanup.
- 2026-04-11 implementation: `server/src/lmstudio/toolService.ts` now assigns queue-overlay precedence so an already-blocked row on a repo stays authoritative when a later waiting request for the same canonical target is still present on disk. The later waiting row no longer replaces the blocked row's `requestId`, `queueState`, `status`, or `queuePosition`.
- 2026-04-11 proof authoring: added direct queue-admission coverage in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-request-queue.test.ts` for cleanup-blocked duplicate reuse plus an explicit assertion that the persisted live-target unique-index filter includes `cleanup-blocked`.

The latest proof commit `61516ef4` then captured that a fresh full `npm run test:summary:server:unit` from current `HEAD` still fails outside any Task 87 proof home in `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-11T21-27-39-743Z.log`, where `not ok 280 - flow stop during a looped flow prevents later iterations from continuing`, wrapper reason `semantic_progress_stalled`, `FLOW_LOOP_OWNERSHIP_RELEASE {"branch":"never_reached","releaseCallCount":0,...}`, and the stop-unwind snapshots all show `pendingCancelRunToken: null` before the bounded queue-owner lane is reached.   External-library and framework precedents confirm that boundary. The required `code_info` lookup was attempted first for repo-pattern research, but the MCP method is unavailable in this environment. Context7 lookup for official Node docs also failed with a transport error, so the proof below uses current repo evidence plus official Node docs, DeepWiki `nodejs/node`, and Context7 `/websockets/ws`. Official Node docs at `https://nodejs.org/download/release/v22.12.0/docs/api/test.html`, `https://nodejs.org/download/release/v22.12.0/docs/api/process.html`, and `https://nodejs.org/download/release/v22.12.0/docs/api/child_process.html` say `node:test` reports extraneous asynchronous activity instead of hiding it, `process.getActiveResourcesInfo()` is the supported way to inspect resources that still keep the event loop alive, and child-process `close` fires only after the child exits and stdio closes. DeepWiki `nodejs/node` confirmed the same test-runner/runtime model and pointed to active-resource diagnosis rather than wrapper-level workarounds. Context7 `/websockets/ws` shows the supported server-side ownership pattern lives at ingress in `wss.on('connection')` and `ws.on('message')`, which matches why the last successful earlier repair lived in `server/src/ws/server.ts` instead of inside the later Task 87 queue-owner files. Exact issue-resolution references line up with that same fix shape: `https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47312280/why-node-js-doesnt-exit-after-promise-call` and `https://stackoverflow.com/questions/18050095/node-js-will-node-always-wait-for-settimeout-to-complete-before-exiting` both explain that active timers or other ref'ed resources, not unresolved Promises alone, keep Node alive. Chosen fix: accept the current repo evidence that Task 87's implementation is still complete but its remaining full-wrapper red path has moved back into the earlier `flows.run.loop` cleanup lane, and hand that contradiction to one fresh bounded prerequisite owner before Task 87 resumes. This fits the current local repo state because the newest full wrapper still never reaches a Task 87 proof failure, while the retained signals point to the same earlier cleanup contradiction again. Rejected alternatives are not suitable: continuing Task 87 implementation would be dishonest because the failure no longer overlaps its owner files; changing `scripts/test-summary-server-unit.mjs` or `scripts/summary-wrapper-protocol.mjs` would only hide the real child-side owner; marking Testing 2 complete would be false because the current full wrapper is red; and `process.exit()`, `--test-force-exit`, arbitrary sleeps, or looser timeout budgets are anti-patterns that mask the earlier prerequisite instead of naming and repairing it.

Re-reading the supported runtime surfaces on current disk shows only `/ingest/start`, `/ingest/reembed/:root`, `/ingest/remove/:root`, and `/ingest/roots` for this task's live queue path, while the only repo-backed way to materialize the required `cleanup-blocked` state for duplicate-owner and overlay proof is the test-only Mongo seeding step in `server/src/test/steps/ingest-manage.steps.ts`. Because Task 87's exit criteria require proving `cleanup-blocked` duplicate ownership and `/ingest/roots` blocked-row precedence, and there is no supported runtime setup path to create that state without test-only database seeding or harness instrumentation, manual proof cannot be completed honestly from the current repository-supported surfaces. No screenshots were captured because no supported browser-visible cleanup-blocked state could be reached without inventing setup.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** 2026-04-11 current manual-proof research proves the right technical answer is not to invent a new runtime seam for Task 87. Repository precedents first: current repo code only exposes `/ingest/start`, `/ingest/reembed/:root`, `/ingest/remove/:root`, and `/ingest/roots` as supported live queue surfaces in `server/src/routes/ingestStart.ts`, `server/src/routes/ingestReembed.ts`, `server/src/routes/ingestRemove.ts`, and `server/src/routes/ingestRoots.ts`. None of those routes can deliberately force the internal delete failure needed to create `cleanup-blocked`; that state is produced only inside queue cleanup ownership in `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts` and is exercised in repo proof homes by direct harness setup, especially the explicit Cucumber seeding step `ingest manage mongo queue has cleanup-blocked request ...` in `server/src/test/steps/ingest-manage.steps.ts` and the matching feature coverage in `server/src/test/features/ingest-roots.feature` and `server/src/test/features/ingest-status.feature`. Story 55 already has one close repo precedent for this exact class of setup problem: the earlier Task 10 blocker answer and repair moved impossible UI-only cleanup work into a dedicated non-user-facing test seam instead of pretending the product route could own it.

  External-library and framework precedents confirm that shape. The required `code_info` lookup was attempted first for repo-pattern research, but the MCP method is unavailable in this environment. DeepWiki research against `mongodb/node-mongodb-native` confirms the supported way to simulate write or delete failures in the MongoDB driver ecosystem is server failpoints such as `configureFailPoint` / `failCommand`, not normal application routes. Official MongoDB docs for `db.adminCommand()` likewise document those commands as administrative operations run against the `admin` database, which fits test or operator fault injection rather than a user-facing product path. DeepWiki research against `microsoft/playwright` and official Playwright API testing docs both say that when browser-visible testing needs server-side state that is awkward or impossible to create through the UI, the recommended pattern is to prepare that state through API calls or fixtures instead of forcing everything through browser actions alone.

  Exact issue-resolution references line up with the same fix. In practice, engineers resolve this class of failure-state proof by using admin/test fixtures or API-backed setup rather than inventing a new user flow: Playwright's official API testing docs explicitly recommend “prepare server state via API calls,” and the `APIRequestContext` docs describe configuring services or preparing the environment for e2e tests. The MongoDB driver precedent is even narrower: simulated delete or write failures are injected through failpoints, which are admin/test mechanisms, not product routes. That matches current local repo evidence exactly: the only reliable way this repo currently reaches `cleanup-blocked` is seeded queue state in the test harness, not a supported main-stack reproduction path.

  Chosen fix: keep Task 87's automated proof as the honest proof owner and treat the current manual proof blocker as a plan-shape issue, not as missing queue implementation. This fits the current local repo state because current `HEAD` already has the task-owned code repaired and both wrapper proofs green, while the remaining manual-proof ask depends on a state that the supported product/runtime surfaces cannot create without test-only seeding or admin-only fault injection. Rejected alternatives are not suitable: do not seed Mongo directly during “manual” proof, do not add an undocumented route just to manufacture `cleanup-blocked`, do not rely on screenshots of unrelated waiting rows, and do not use ad hoc failpoint or shell commands outside a repo-owned seam. Those options would invent unsupported setup instead of proving the story through the surfaces this repository actually supports.

Task 87 therefore remains `__in_progress__` only so the normal final audit path can close it honestly without reintroducing an unsupported manual-proof gate.

### Task 89. Harden The E2E Cleanup Route To Known-Root Ownership

- Repository Name: `Current Repository`
- Task Dependencies: `88`
- Task Status: `__done__`
- Notes: Added after review pass `0000055-20260411T104227Z-756a77d1` found that the e2e cleanup route forwards a caller-controlled `:root` selector into destructive queue and root deletion without a known-root ownership check. Re-owned to `__to_do__` on 2026-04-12 by planner repair after the current earlier-lane prerequisite again proved a fresh `flows.run.loop` owner must land before this task's remaining full-wrapper proof can continue honestly.

#### Overview

This review-fix task narrows the e2e cleanup route back to its intended authority boundary. The route may stay available for the shared e2e stack, but it must only delete queue/root state for the mounted e2e fixture root `/fixtures/repo` and descendants under that root instead of blindly trusting any normalized path string a caller provides.

#### Task Exit Criteria

- The e2e cleanup route rejects caller-supplied roots that are not under the approved mounted e2e fixture root `/fixtures/repo`.
- `deleteWaitingQueueRequestsByTargetPath()` and `removeRoot()` are only reached for approved known roots.
- Direct tests cover both allowed fixture-root cleanup and rejected unknown-root cleanup requests in the same route test file.
- The route contract and any supporting env/config seam remain explicit and documented in code, tests, and the maintained summary.

#### Documentation Locations

- `planning/0000055-users-can-queue-ingest-and-re-embed-requests.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260411T104227Z-756a77d1-findings.md`
- `server/src/routes/ingestE2eCleanup.ts`
- `server/src/ingest/requestContracts.ts`
- `server/src/ingest/ingestJob.ts`
- `server/.env.e2e`
- `server/src/test/integration/ingest-e2e-cleanup.test.ts`

#### Proof Mapping

- Allowed cleanup for `/fixtures/repo` and its descendants: owned by `server/src/routes/ingestE2eCleanup.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/integration/ingest-e2e-cleanup.test.ts`.
- Unknown-root rejection before `deleteWaitingQueueRequestsByTargetPath()` runs: owned by `server/src/routes/ingestE2eCleanup.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/integration/ingest-e2e-cleanup.test.ts`.
- Unknown-root rejection before `removeRoot()` runs: owned by `server/src/routes/ingestE2eCleanup.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/integration/ingest-e2e-cleanup.test.ts`.
- Route rejection contract for normalized-but-outside paths such as `/fixtures/repo/../outside` or `/tmp/not-allowed`: owned by `server/src/routes/ingestE2eCleanup.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/integration/ingest-e2e-cleanup.test.ts`.

4. [x] Add an approved-root validation helper or equivalent guard in `server/src/routes/ingestE2eCleanup.ts` that accepts only `/fixtures/repo` and descendants. Purpose: make the route's allowed-root contract explicit in code.
5. [x] Gate `deleteWaitingQueueRequestsByTargetPath()` behind that approved-root validation. Purpose: prevent queue deletion for unknown roots.
6. [x] Gate `removeRoot()` behind that approved-root validation. Purpose: prevent destructive root deletion for unknown roots.
7. [x] Add the explicit rejected-route response shape for unknown roots in `server/src/routes/ingestE2eCleanup.ts`. Purpose: make the rejection path inspectable and testable.
8. [x] Test type: server integration. Location: `server/src/test/integration/ingest-e2e-cleanup.test.ts`. Description: prove the allowed fixture-root path under `/fixtures/repo` still succeeds through the cleanup route. Purpose: preserve the intended e2e cleanup capability while the guard tightens.
9. [x] Test type: server integration. Location: `server/src/test/integration/ingest-e2e-cleanup.test.ts`. Description: prove an unknown root such as `/tmp/not-allowed` is rejected before `deleteWaitingQueueRequestsByTargetPath()` runs. Purpose: make the first destructive-boundary guard explicit instead of implied.
10. [x] Test type: server integration. Location: `server/src/test/integration/ingest-e2e-cleanup.test.ts`. Description: prove the same unknown-root request is rejected before `removeRoot()` runs. Purpose: give the second destructive-boundary guard its own proof home.

#### Implementation notes

- Inserted on 2026-04-12 by planner repair because Task 94's renewed live `**BLOCKER**` plus current `**BLOCKING ANSWER**` proved the latest full `server:unit` rerun from current `HEAD` regressed earlier again to the shared `flows.run.loop` cleanup lane before any remaining Task 94 proof home is reached. This fresh successor task exists because Task 94's current implementation is already on disk, while the remaining red path again belongs to a different earlier owner.
- Subtask 1: re-read Task 94's current `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`, current `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and retained full-wrapper log `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-12T06-30-21-434Z.log` from disk. Current evidence still anchors this prerequisite to an earlier full-wrapper regression at `not ok 280 - flow stop during a looped flow prevents later iterations from continuing`, not to any remaining Task 94 selector proof home.
- Subtask 2: re-inspected `server/src/test/integration/flows.run.loop.test.ts`, `server/src/flows/service.ts`, `server/src/ws/server.ts`, `server/src/chat/inflightRegistry.ts`, and `server/src/agents/runLock.ts` before rerunning wrappers. The bounded seams still center on pending-cancel consumption, inflight cleanup, and conversation-lock release order rather than on the later re-embed selector files.
- Testing 1 and Subtask 3: reran `npm run test:summary:server:unit -- --file server/src/test/integration/flows.run.loop.test.ts --test-name "flow stop during a looped flow prevents later iterations from continuing"` because Subtask 3 explicitly required the wrapper comparison, and it passed cleanly in `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-12T07-00-50-710Z.log` with `agent_action: skip_log`. The fresh full `npm run test:summary:server:unit` rerun still failed first in the same bounded lane in `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-12T07-01-00-735Z.log`, and the retained failure block now narrows the local contradiction further: repeated `runFlowInstruction.afterCleanupInflight` checkpoints clear inflight successfully, but the stop-unwind never reaches `releaseConversationLockFn`, so ownership stays pinned under the same run token while later loop cleanup continues.
- **RESOLVED ISSUE** Earlier Task 93 blocker rounds exhausted three same-class approaches cleanly and are now historical only: first, the broader finalize/release-order diagnosis proved `cleanupInflight` succeeded while the unwind never reached `releaseConversationLockFn`; second, a narrower pre-handoff workaround that treated a consumed pending stop as terminal even when `abortInflight(...)` returned false also exhausted cleanly and was reverted after the targeted slice stayed green while the full wrapper still failed first in the same lane; third, a pure post-step conversion also exhausted cleanly when the targeted proof home proved the stop token was already gone before that later boundary could act. Those exhausted attempts are superseded by the current `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, which narrows the remaining owner to the destructive consume order inside the earlier pre-handoff helper plus the later stop boundary that still needs that token to survive.
- **BLOCKING ANSWER** The best fix is still to keep Task 93 local, but the latest targeted regression proves the surviving bug is even tighter than the last blocker answer: the pending cancel is being consumed too early, so the later post-step boundary never has a stop token left to convert into `'stopped'`. Repository precedents now point directly at that owner: `server/src/chat/inflightRegistry.ts` already distinguishes binding (`bindPendingConversationCancelToInflight`), consuming (`consumePendingConversationCancel`), and cleanup (`cleanupPendingConversationCancel`) as separate operations; `consumePendingConversationCancel` is destructive and deletes the pending record immediately, while `cleanupPendingConversationCancel` only removes it when the caller is sure the matched run/inflight should clear it. In current `server/src/flows/service.ts`, `consumePendingFlowStop()` binds the pending stop, then immediately calls `consumePendingConversationCancel(...)` before attempting `abortInflight(...)`, so when `abortInflight(...)` returns false the stop token is already gone. That exactly matches the current retained proof: both the full-wrapper log `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-12T07-26-11-162Z.log` and the targeted-regression log `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-12T07-48-41-841Z.log` show `FLOW_LOOP_OWNERSHIP_RELEASE {"branch":"never_reached",...}` plus repeated `runFlowInstruction.afterCleanupInflight`, `runFlowInstruction.afterCleanupPendingConversationCancel` with `cleared=false shouldClear=false`, and `runFlowInstruction.returnResult` with `status=ok`, while the runtime snapshots at those checkpoints already show `pendingCancelRunToken=null`. That means the failed post-step conversion was looking in the right general place but too late to see the signal, because the earlier pre-handoff helper had already deleted it even on the `abortInflight(...) === false` path. The concrete fix that fits current repo state is therefore: keep the current step-boundary stop propagation model, but change `consumePendingFlowStop()` so a pending stop is not destructively consumed unless the abort actually succeeds, or otherwise re-preserve the bound pending stop for the same run/inflight when abort cannot act yet. Once the token survives that earlier branch, the later post-step boundary can honestly observe it and convert the successful step return into `'stopped'`, after which the existing `shouldStopAfter(...)`, `runSteps`, `runStartLoopStep`, and `runFlowUnlocked.finalize` owners can unwind and release ownership without inventing a second release path. External-library confirmation: DeepWiki and Context7-backed official Node docs both confirm that `node:test` defaults to per-file process isolation and the wrapper should treat the first failing file as the honest owner, while the official `child_process` docs and the local summary wrapper both use child-process close semantics to determine completion, so there is no support for blaming a later green task instead. Issue-resolution research: targeted external search on “passes alone, fails in suite” behavior still points engineers back to shared-state and cleanup-order bugs in the earliest shared owner, and this repo’s own bind-versus-consume helper split gives the concrete mechanism for fixing that class of bug here. Rejected alternatives: do not retry the widened pre-handoff `abortInflight(...)` workaround because it already exhausted cleanly; do not retry the pure post-step conversion by itself because the latest targeted proof disproved it while the token was still being deleted too early; do not patch Task 94 again because its targeted proof homes remain green and the full wrapper still fails earlier; do not add an ad-hoc `releaseConversationLock()` in `runFlowInstruction` cleanup because repo precedent centralizes ownership release in finalizers and a second release would hide the real destructive-consume bug; do not rewrite `cleanupInflight()` or the lock helper first because current logs prove cleanup completes and the release callback is never reached. I attempted the required `code_info` pattern lookup first, but the MCP method is unavailable in this environment (`Method not found`), so the proof relies on current repo evidence, DeepWiki plus Context7 official runtime documentation, targeted web issue-resolution research, and the local wrapper code.
- **RESOLVED ISSUE** The destructive-consume owner is now closed. Preserving the bound pending stop until `abortInflight(...)` actually succeeds, then letting the later post-step boundary consume it, restored the Task 93-local stop propagation path without reopening the older exhausted branches.
- Subtask 4 and Testing 2: repaired `server/src/flows/service.ts` so `consumePendingFlowStop()` no longer clears a bound pending stop before `abortInflight(...)` can actually act, then restored the shared post-step pending-cancel conversion so a successful step can still flip to `stopped` when that preserved token survives to the later boundary. The exact targeted rerun `npm run test:summary:server:unit -- --file server/src/test/integration/flows.run.loop.test.ts --test-name "flow stop during a looped flow prevents later iterations from continuing"` passed cleanly in `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-12T08-08-37-851Z.log` with `agent_action: skip_log`, and the fresh full `npm run test:summary:server:unit` rerun no longer fails first in `flows.run.loop`; it now advances into the later queue-request reuse cluster at `not ok 898` through `not ok 901` in `test-results/server-unit-tests-2026-04-12T08-08-52-331Z.log`, led by `concurrent first-submit requests collapse onto one waiting queue row`, `running duplicate reuse returns the existing running queue item without mutating active settings`, `cleanup-blocked duplicate reuse returns the blocked queue item instead of creating a later waiting owner`, and `waiting queue position counts only older waiting items and uses countDocuments instead of loading the full queue`. That closes this bounded loop-cleanup branch honestly and hands execution forward to the later queue-request owner instead of widening Task 93 again.
- Automated-proof audit: normalized Task 93 to `__done__` because all subtasks and testing are honestly complete on current disk, no live standalone `**BLOCKER**` remains, and the retained full-wrapper proof now advances into Task 94's later queue-request reuse cluster instead of leaving any local Task 93 gate open.

### Task 95. Restrict Re-Embed Selectors To Already-Ingested Roots

- Repository Name: `Current Repository`
- Task Dependencies: `94`
- Task Status: `__done__`
- Notes: Added after review pass `0000055-20260411T104227Z-756a77d1` found that queued start-ingest rows temporarily synthesized into the repo list can be selected for re-embed even though they were never ingested. Re-owned to `__to_do__` again on 2026-04-12 by planner repair after this task's renewed live blocker proved a fresh earlier `flows.run.loop` prerequisite must land before its remaining full-wrapper proof can continue honestly.

#### Overview

This review-fix task restores the re-embed contract that `sourceId` must identify an already-ingested repository root. Story 55 intentionally surfaced queued start rows before first ingest completes; those temporary rows still need visibility, but they must not become legal re-embed targets or be able to rewrite a queued `start` request into `reembed`.

#### Task Exit Criteria

- Re-embed selectors and route lookups reject temporary queued start rows that do not correspond to an already-ingested repository root.
- A queued start row may remain visible in the repo list, but it is excluded from re-embed selection and from any legal re-embed submission payload until the root has actually been ingested.
- Waiting duplicate updates cannot silently rewrite a queued `start` request into `reembed` for a never-ingested row.
- Selector narrowing stays on the existing bounded ingested-listing path instead of adding per-row lookup or unbounded rescans just to exclude never-ingested queued rows.
- Direct tests cover selector-list output, direct route targeting, and queued duplicate-rewrite protection for never-ingested roots.
- The repo-list visibility contract for queued start rows remains intact while re-embed targeting is narrowed.

#### Documentation Locations

- `planning/0000055-users-can-queue-ingest-and-re-embed-requests.md`
- `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260411T104227Z-756a77d1-findings.md`
- `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts`
- `server/src/routes/ingestReembed.ts`
- `server/src/ingest/requestQueue.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/reingestService.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/reingestExecution.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/ingest-reembed.test.ts`
- `server/src/test/unit/tools-ingested-repos.test.ts`

#### Proof Mapping

- Re-embed selector lists exclude never-ingested queued start rows: owned by `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/reingestService.test.ts`.
- Direct route targeting rejects a stale submitted `sourceId` copied from a still-visible never-ingested queued row instead of resolving it as a re-embed source: owned by `server/src/routes/ingestReembed.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/reingestExecution.test.ts`.
- Selector narrowing keeps using the existing bounded listing snapshot instead of issuing per-row re-query behavior for queued start rows: owned by `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts` and request-scoped execution helpers; prove in `server/src/test/unit/reingestExecution.test.ts`.
- Waiting duplicate updates cannot rewrite queued `start` work into `reembed` for a never-ingested root: owned by `server/src/ingest/requestQueue.ts`; prove in `server/src/test/unit/ingest-reembed.test.ts`.
- Repo-list visibility for queued start rows remains intact while re-embed targeting narrows: owned by the shared repo-list builder surface; prove in `server/src/test/unit/tools-ingested-repos.test.ts`.

#### Subtasks

1. [x] Re-read the re-embed selector finding in `codeInfoStatus/reviews/0000055-20260411T104227Z-756a77d1-findings.md`. Purpose: keep the task anchored to the never-ingested queued-row defect.
2. [x] Inspect `buildRetryLists(...)` in `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts`. Purpose: identify where queued start rows are currently treated as reingestable.
3. [x] Inspect the direct route lookup in `server/src/routes/ingestReembed.ts`. Purpose: identify the route seam that must reject never-ingested queued rows.
4. [x] Inspect the waiting-duplicate update path in `server/src/ingest/requestQueue.ts`. Purpose: identify where queued `start` work can currently be rewritten into `reembed`.
5. [x] Inspect the existing listing-snapshot or selector-fetch path used by `buildRetryLists(...)` and direct execution helpers. Purpose: confirm where bounded reuse of the ingested listing would drift if queued-start filtering added per-row lookups.
6. [x] Update `server/src/ingest/reingestService.ts` so `buildRetryLists(...)` exposes only already-ingested roots as reembeddable. Purpose: keep queued start visibility without widening re-embed eligibility.
